title: Inverse normal-form metric
summary: Defines the expected inverse block components of the normal-form metric and proves the base-block left-inverse identity from an explicit inverse hypothesis.

mathematicalContent.heading: Inverse component matrix
mathematicalContent.body: Given a formal inverse gⁱʲ of the tangential block gᵢⱼ and t ≠ 0, the normal-form inverse metric has the displayed block matrix in the ordered directions (0,i,∞).

mathematicalContent.heading: Verified scope
mathematicalContent.body: The file stores gⁱʲ as data and states left- and right-inverse conditions separately. It proves only the base–base contribution of the left inverse, under t ≠ 0 and the supplied base left-inverse condition; it does not prove the full ambient matrix inverse.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This extends normal metric components by a proposed inverse tangential block gⁱʲ; inverse identities are not included in the structure itself.
correspondence.leanType: structure NormalInverseMetricComponents (ι : Type w) extends NormalMetricComponents ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This definition assigns the expected inverse normal-form component to each pair of ambient indices.
correspondence.leanType: def inverseMetricComp (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : AmbientIndex ι → AmbientIndex ι → ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The contravariant scale–scale component is zero.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_zero_zero (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero AmbientIndex.zero = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The scale–transverse inverse component is t⁻¹.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_zero_inf (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero AmbientIndex.inf = C.t⁻¹

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The transposed transverse–scale inverse component is t⁻¹.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_inf_zero (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf AmbientIndex.zero = C.t⁻¹

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The inverse tangential block is t⁻² times the proposed inverse gⁱʲ of the slice metric.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_base_base (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) (i j : ι) : inverseMetricComp C (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = (C.t ^ 2)⁻¹ * C.gInv i j
theorem inverseMetricComp_inf_inf (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf AmbientIndex.inf = -2 * C.rho * (C.t ^ 2)⁻¹

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The proposed gⁱʲ is a left inverse of gᵢⱼ when contracting its second index with the first index of g gives the Kronecker delta.
correspondence.leanType: def BaseLeftInverse [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The proposed gⁱʲ is a right inverse when the contraction in the opposite order gives the Kronecker delta.
correspondence.leanType: def BaseRightInverse [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Assuming t ≠ 0 and BaseLeftInverse, the contraction of the ambient inverse and metric over base indices reduces to the ordinary base inverse identity.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverse_metric_base_base_left [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) (ht : C.t ≠ 0) (hbase : BaseLeftInverse C) (i j : ι) : (∑ k : ι, inverseMetricComp C (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base k) * ambientMetricComp C.toNormalMetricComponents (AmbientIndex.base k) (AmbientIndex.base j)) = AmbientIndex.kronecker (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j)

references.locator: Chapter 3, inverse metric in normal-form coordinates
references.note: Source for the inverse block matrix used in component calculations.

limitations: The inverse base block is supplied as data. Only one base–base left-contraction identity is proved; the complete ambient inverse identity, nondegeneracy, and the right inverse are not established here.
